bucket: Force Point and Force Power Based Feats

implemented.mechanic: Adds +3 to Force Point maximum through normalized resourceRules.forcePoints MAX_BONUS and ForcePointsService max calculation.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointsService.getMax uses the Force Point feat rule reader and legacy MetaResource resolver without double-counting.

implemented.mechanic: Uses d8 Force Point dice for attack, skill, and ability check adjustment contexts.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointsService.getScalingDice/getDieSize now accepts Force Point spend context, and ForcePointsUtil passes that context into rolls/dialogs.

implemented.mechanic: Allows off-turn/reaction Force Point spending as a Free Action while preserving normal spend restrictions.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointFeatRules.canSpendOffTurn and ForcePointsService validation enforce off-turn spending only when this rule is present or a caller supplies an equivalent rule.

implemented.mechanic: Once per encounter helper grants 1 temporary Force Point when an allied Force Power or Force Talent affects the actor without damage or condition-track loss.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointFeatActions.grantJediFamiliarity writes a bonusForcePoints pool entry consumed before normal Force Points by ActorEngine.spendForcePoints.
implemented.boundary: Automatic triggering depends on Force Power/Talent workflows calling the helper with ally/effect context.

implemented.mechanic: Provides max(1, floor(Dark Side Score / 2)) bonus for Use the Force checks made to resist Sense Force detection.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointFeatRules.getSenseForceDetectionResistanceBonus returns the bonus for Sense Force detection-resistance contexts.
implemented.boundary: Callsites must request this helper in Sense Force resistance workflows.

implemented.mechanic: +5 insight bonus to Fortitude and Will Defense against attacks/effects requiring a Use the Force check.
implemented.boundary: Defense resolution callsites must request this helper when resolving Use the Force attacks/effects.

implemented.mechanic: Successful Gather Information / Learn Secret Information grants 1 normal Force Point, capped at 3 per level and normal Force Point max.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointFeatActions.grantConfidentSuccess uses ActorEngine.gainForcePoints and a per-level flag to enforce the feat cap.

implemented.mechanic: Natural 20 on a Pilot check grants 1 temporary Force Point until encounter end.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointFeatActions.grantSpacersSurge writes a temporary bonusForcePoints pool entry.

implemented.mechanic: When spending a Force Point on an Atlatl or Cesta attack roll, increases the FP die type one step, capped at d10.
implemented.runtime: ForcePointFeatRules.getForcePointDieSize applies weapon-context DIE_STEP rules, and ForcePointsUtil passes weapon/roll context into ForcePointsService.

implemented.mechanic: For Force Training power entitlement only, treats the configured Force Training ability score as 4 higher before deriving the modifier and applying the minimum of 1 power.
implemented.runtime: force-training-entitlement-runtime-patches.js patches FeatGrantEntitlementResolver.getForceTrainingSlotsPerInstance to calculate max(1, 1 + modifier(configured ability + 4)) when Jedi Heritage applies, respecting the house-rule setting that swaps WIS to CHA.
implemented.notes: This is intentionally not a fixed +2 powers. Low WIS/CHA values can produce +0 or +1 compared to the unmodified entitlement because the minimum is applied after the adjusted ability modifier is calculated.

notes: Temporary Force Point feats use the existing flags.swse.bonusForcePoints pool, which ActorEngine.spendForcePoints already consumes before normal Force Points.
ForcePointServiceRuntime patches validation so temporary/bonus Force Points count for spend eligibility, matching ActorEngine spend behavior.
This pass intentionally exposes helper/rule APIs for event-specific feats where fully automatic triggering requires workflow-specific context not present in every legacy caller.
Jedi Heritage applies only to Force Training power entitlement calculation; it does not mutate the actor's WIS/CHA score or affect other derived values.
